The essentials

Race Info

Everything you need for race weekend — where it starts, where it ends, and how to get your bib.

Start — Eldorado

La Tienda Plaza, Eldorado (7 Caliente Rd), elevation 6,992 ft.

Point-to-point via Old Las Vegas Highway. Gun at 7:30 AM MDT — mornings up here are crisp, so dress for the start, not the finish.

Finish — Railyard Park

Railyard Park (740 Cerrillos Rd), elevation 6,956 ft, in the heart of Santa Fe.

Course cutoff is 3 hours 30 minutes. Live results post as you cross the line.

Packet Pickup & Expo

Expo and packet pickup at Old Warehouse 21, next to Railyard Park:
Friday 12–6 PM and Saturday 10 AM–5 PM.

Race-morning pickup 5:45–7:10 AM at the Eldorado start.

The course

Elevation Profile

Point-to-point and net-downhill: start at 6,992 ft in Eldorado, climb to the 7,330 ft high point at the Woods Loop near mile 4, then descend to the Railyard finish at 6,956 ft. Hover the chart for mile-by-mile elevations.

6,956′Finish
net −36′Downhill

How results work

  • On race day — chip times post live as you finish; the leaderboard refreshes every 30 seconds.
  • Find your time — search by bib number in the leaderboard the moment your result posts.
  • Questions? — results are official once posted by timing. Find us at the results tent by the finish.

Race weekend

Where to Stay

Our lodging partner is holding a race-week deal for Santa Fe Half Marathon runners.

Pecos Trail Inn

Santa Fe Half Marathon runners save 15% on race-week stays — Wednesday, Sept 16 through Tuesday, Sept 22. Mention the promo code when you book:

The expo

Vendor Booths

Join us at Old Warehouse 21, next to Railyard Park, for race-weekend expo & packet pickup. One flat fee covers your booth for the whole weekend.

$300

Flat vendor fee — covers both expo days. No per-day tiers.

Expo days & hours

Both days are included in your fee. When you apply, the day checkboxes just tell us when you plan to be on the floor — they don't change your price.

Friday, Sept 1812–6 PM
Saturday, Sept 1910 AM–5 PM
Apply for a booth

Racing at 7,000 feet

Respect the Altitude

Santa Fe sits higher than Denver. The thin air is part of the challenge — and the charm. A little planning goes a long way.

Arrive early if you can

Coming from low elevation? Two to three days in town before the race helps your body adjust — and Santa Fe is a fine place to spend them.

Hydrate all week

The high desert is dry and you lose more fluid than you notice. Keep a water bottle in hand for a few days before the gun.

Pace the first four miles

The climb to the 7,330 ft high point comes early. Run it a touch slower than goal pace — the net-downhill back half is where you make it up.

Good to know

Frequently Asked Questions

The short answers, all in one place. Everything here is covered in more detail up the page.

When and where does the race start?

Sunday, September 20, 2026, at 7:30 AM Mountain time, from La Tienda Plaza in Eldorado (7 Caliente Rd). It's a point-to-point course via Old Las Vegas Highway, finishing at Railyard Park (740 Cerrillos Rd) in the heart of Santa Fe.

How long is the course, really?

13.109 miles, USATF-certified, and net-downhill: you start at 6,992 ft, top out at 7,330 ft near mile 4, and finish at 6,956 ft. See the full elevation profile.

Is there a time limit?

Yes — 3 hours 30 minutes.

Where do I pick up my packet?

At the expo at Old Warehouse 21, next to Railyard Park: Friday 12–6 PM and Saturday 10 AM–5 PM. Race-morning pickup runs 5:45–7:10 AM at the Eldorado start.

How do I register?

On RunSignup — registration is open now.

How do I find my time after I finish?

Chip times post live as you cross the line. Open the live leaderboard and type your bib number into "Find my time" — past editions are on RunSignup results.

Will there be race photos?

Yes, free ones — from the start line in Eldorado to the Railyard finish. Links to all the galleries are in the Photos section.

I live at sea level. How bad is the altitude?

You'll feel it — Santa Fe sits higher than Denver. Arrive a couple of days early if you can, hydrate all week, and pace the first four miles gently. Full advice in Respect the Altitude.

Is there a deal on lodging?

Yes — the Pecos Trail Inn gives runners 15% off race-week stays (Wed, Sept 16 through Tue, Sept 22) with code RUNSANTAFE26. Details in Where to Stay.

Can my business have a booth at the expo?

Yes — one flat $300 fee covers both expo days at Old Warehouse 21. See Vendor Booths.
